data.title: Douglas Emmett Announces Dates for Its 2025 First Quarter Earnings Results and Live Conference Call
data.description:
Douglas Emmett Announces Dates for Its 2025 First Quarter Earnings Results and Live Conference Call

SANTA MONICA, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 03, 2025--

Douglas Emmett, Inc. , a real estate investment trust (REIT), announced today that it plans to release its 2025 first quarter earnings results after market close on Tuesday, May 6, 2025. A live conference call is scheduled for the following day, Wednesday, May 7, 2025, at 11:00 a.m. Pacific Time / 2:00 p.m. Eastern Time. Jordan Kaplan, President and Chief Executive Officer, will host the call along with Peter Seymour, Chief Financial Officer, Kevin Crummy, Chief Investment Officer, and Stuart McElhinney, Vice President Investor Relations. Interested parties can listen to the call via the following:

INTERNET: Go to www.douglasemmett.com/investors at least fifteen minutes prior to the start time of the call in order to register, download and install any necessary audio software.

PHONE: 888-349-0488 (U.S.) or 412-542-4156 (International). Please ask to join the Douglas Emmett, Inc. call.

REPLAY: A rebroadcast of the live call will be available for 90 days on our website at www.douglasemmett.com/investors

About Douglas Emmett, Inc.

Douglas Emmett, Inc. (DEI) is a fully integrated, self-administered and self-managed real estate investment trust (REIT), and one of the largest owners and operators of high-quality office and multifamily properties located in the premier coastal submarkets of Los Angeles and Honolulu. Douglas Emmett focuses on owning and acquiring a substantial share of top-tier office properties and premier multifamily communities in neighborhoods that possess significant supply constraints, high-end executive housing and key lifestyle amenities. For more information about Douglas Emmett, please visit our website at www.douglasemmett.com.

Safe Harbor Statement

Except for the historical facts, the statements in this press release regarding Douglas Emmett's business activities are forward-looking statements based on the beliefs of, assumptions made by, and information currently available to us about known and unknown risks, trends, uncertainties and factors that are beyond our control or ability to predict. Although we believe that our assumptions are reasonable, they are not guarantees of future performance and some will inevitably prove to be incorrect. As a result, our actual future results can be expected to differ from our expectations, and those differences may be material. Accordingly, investors should use caution in relying on forward-looking statements to anticipate future results or trends. For a discussion of some of the risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ from those contained in the forward-looking statements, see "Risk Factors" in our Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250403380142/en/

CONTACT: Stuart McElhinney, Vice President — Investor Relations

310.255.7751 smcelhinney@douglasemmett.com

data.title: Class Action Announcement APP: A Securities Fraud Class Action Lawsuit Was Filed Against AppLovin Corporation (APP)
data.description:
RADNOR, Pa., April 03, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — The law firm of Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP (www.ktmc.com) informs investors that securities class action lawsuits have been filed in the United States District Court for the Northern District of California against AppLovin Corporation (“AppLovin”) (NASDAQ: APP) on behalf of those who purchased or otherwise acquired AppLovin securities between May 10, 2023, and February 25, 2025, inclusive (the “Class Period”). The lead plaintiff deadline is May 5, 2025.

CONTACT KESSLER TOPAZ MELTZER & CHECK, LLP:

If you suffered AppLovin losses, you may CLICK HERE or copy and paste the following link into your browser: https://www.ktmc.com/new-cases/applovin-corporation?utm_source=PR&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=app&mktm=r

You can also contact attorney Jonathan Naji, Esq. by calling (484) 270-1453 or by email at info@ktmc.com.

DEFENDANTS’ ALLEGED MISCONDUCT:

The complaints allege that, throughout the Class Period, Defendants made materially false and/or misleading statements, as well as failed to disclose material facts about the company’s business, operations, and prospects. Specifically, Defendants misrepresented and/or failed to disclose that: (1) AppLovin’s app segment relied on the systematic exploitation of fraudulent advertising practices including “clickjacking” and “click spoofing”; (2) AppLovin’s advertising and e-commerce program relied on intercepting and appropriating advertising attribution credit; (3) AppLovin employed a backdoor installation scheme to force unwanted apps on customers; (4) AppLovin’s revenue was falsely inflated; and (5) as a result, Defendants’ statements about the company’s business, operations, and prospects were materially false and misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis at all relevant times.

THE LEAD PLAINTIFF PROCESS:

AppLovin investors may, no later than May 5, 2025, seek to be appointed as a lead plaintiff representative of the class through Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP or other counsel, or may choose to do nothing and remain an absent class member. A lead plaintiff is a representative party who acts on behalf of all class members in directing the litigation.  The lead plaintiff is usually the investor or small group of investors who have the largest financial interest and who are also adequate and typical of the proposed class of investors. The lead plaintiff selects counsel to represent the lead plaintiff and the class and these attorneys, if approved by the court, are lead or class counsel. Your ability to share in any recovery is not affected by the decision of whether or not to serve as a lead plaintiff.

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP encourages AppLovin investors who have suffered significant losses to contact the firm directly to acquire more information.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR THE CASE OR GO TO: https://www.ktmc.com/new-cases/applovin-corporation?utm_source=PR&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=app&mktm=r

ABOUT KESSLER TOPAZ MELTZER & CHECK, LLP:     

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP prosecutes class actions in state and federal courts throughout the country and around the world. The firm has developed a global reputation for excellence and has recovered billions of dollars for victims of fraud and other corporate misconduct. All of our work is driven by a common goal: to protect investors, consumers, employees and others from fraud, abuse, misconduct and negligence by businesses and fiduciaries. The complaint in this action was not filed by Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP. For more information about Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP please visit www.ktmc.com.

CONTACT:

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP

Jonathan Naji, Esq.

(484) 270-1453

280 King of Prussia Road

Radnor, PA 19087

info@ktmc.com

May be considered attorney advertising in certain jurisdictions. Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

data.title: Tasmea Raised to Buy From Hold by Shaw & Partners >TEA.AU
ASX Small-Cap Stocks to Watch
data.description:
Investors are likely to keep an eye on the following small-cap stocks listed on the Australian Securities Exchange.

Swift Networks Group

Swift Networks Group clients Roy Hill and Pilbara Minerals renewed their Swift subscriptions for a further 12-month period, totaling AU$1.3 million.

Bayan Mining and Minerals

Bayan Mining and Minerals started a field program at its Bayan Springs project in Nevada, US to evaluate high-priority targets identified through hyperspectral imagery analysis and desktop studies.

Assetora

Assetora partnered with property portfolio manager EquitiFund to manage a mortgage portfolio valued at around AU$350 million and up to AU$700 million for an initial five-year term.

Blue Energy

Blue Energy obtained an amended environmental authority from the Land Court of Queensland for its subsidiary Eureka Petroleum to conduct petroleum activities on the Sapphire petroleum lease 1034 application area.

Inca Minerals

Inca Minerals said its off-market takeover bid for all shares in Stunalara Metals will close Friday at 5 pm Australian Western Standard Time. It plans to proceed with the compulsory acquisition of any outstanding Stunalara Metals shares.

Roblox Corp Director, Christopher Carvalho, sold 12,985 shares of Class A Common Stock on April 1, 2025, under a Rule 10b5-1 Plan. data.title: PubMatic Executives Sell Shares to Cover Tax Obligations
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Goel Amar K.CHAIRMAN, CHIEF INNOVATION OFFSell$91,175Form 4
Woods AndrewGENERAL COUNSEL & SECRETARYSell$61,802Form 4
Klimenko PaulinaCHIEF GROWTH OFFICERSell$70,958Form 4
Kumar MukulPRESIDENT, ENGINEERINGSell$50,268Form 4

Several executives at PubMatic, a leading advertising technology company, have recently sold shares of Class A Common Stock to cover tax withholding obligations related to the vesting of restricted stock units (RSUs).

Amar K. Goel, Chairman and Chief Innovation Officer, sold a total of 10,351 shares on April 2 and April 3, 2025, at weighted average prices of $9.3073 and $8.4327, respectively, totaling $91,175. Following these transactions, Goel directly owns 12,496 shares.

Andrew Woods, General Counsel & Secretary, sold 6,902 shares on April 2 and April 3, 2025, at weighted average prices of $9.3073 and $8.4345, respectively, totaling $61,802. Woods now directly owns 36,718 shares.

Paulina Klimenko, Chief Growth Officer, sold 7,624 shares on April 2, 2025, at a weighted average price of $9.3073, totaling $70,958. Klimenko's direct ownership post-transaction is 38,592 shares.

Kumar Mukul, PRESIDENT, ENGINEERING, sold 5,401 shares on April 2, 2025, at a weighted average price of $9.3073, totaling $50,268. Mukul directly owns 60,465 shares following the sale.

Depo-Provera (DP) and generic equivalent (DMPA) investigated by Federman & Sherwood
data.description:
OKLAHOMA CITY, Okla., April 03, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Depo-Provera (DP) and generic equivalent (DMPA) investigated by Federman & Sherwood. Federman & Sherwood, a premier boutique class-action litigation law firm based in Oklahoma City and Dallas announced that it is investigating cases related to women’s use of Depo-Provera (DP) and its generic equivalent (DMPA). DP is used as an injectable birth control and to manage menstrual cycles. At high doses it can be used to treat certain types of cancer. Its use has been linked to possibly cause brain tumors including meningioma brain tumors. This new evidence is supporting lawsuits that seek to compensate women for medical monitoring and financial compensation. If you took or know someone who took Depo-Provera you should find out what your rights are to seek financial compensation.

Federman & Sherwood is committed to providing aggressive legal representation to recover financial compensation for women harmed by use of DP or its generic equivalent.

If you wish to discuss this action, obtain further information, and/or participate in this litigation, please contact Tashia Poore either by email at tdp@federmanlaw.com or visit our firm’s website www.federmanlaw.com.

Tashia Poore

FEDERMAN & SHERWOOD

(405) 235-1560

data.title: Checkpoint Therapeutics Executives Sell Shares for Tax Obligations
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Gray William GarrettChief Financial OfficerSell$49,904Form 4
Oliviero James F IiiCEO, President and DirectorSell$60,963Form 4

Checkpoint Therapeutics executives, including the Chief Financial Officer and the CEO, have recently sold shares of the company's common stock to cover tax obligations related to the vesting of restricted shares.

William Garrett Gray, the CFO, sold 12,322 shares at a weighted average price of $4.05 per share, totaling $49,904, to satisfy tax withholding obligations. Following this sale, Gray directly owns 1,446,322 shares of Checkpoint Therapeutics' common stock, including shares of restricted common stock that vest over time.

James F. Oliviero III, the CEO, President, and Director, sold 15,090 shares at a price of $4.04 per share, totaling $60,963, for the same purpose. After this transaction, Oliviero directly owns 3,759,929 shares of the company's common stock, which also includes shares of restricted common stock that vest over various time periods.

data.title: Shareholders that Lost Money on Neumora Therapeutics, Inc. (NMRA) Urged to Join Class Action - Contact Levi & Korsinsky to Learn More
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 3, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Neumora Therapeutics, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/neumora-therapeutics-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=140874&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: This lawsuit is on behalf of a class of all persons or entities who purchased or otherwise acquired Neumora common stock pursuant and/or traceable to the Offering Documents, commenced on or about September 15, 2023.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (1) in order for Neumora to justify conducting its Phase Three Program, Neumora was forced to amend BlackThorn's original Phase Two trial inclusion criteria to include a patient population with moderate to severe Major Depressive Disorder, MDD, to show that Navacaprant, Neumora's flagship therapeutic candidate, offered a statistically significant improvement in treating MDD; (2) and to that same end, the Company also added a prespecified analysis to the Phase Two statistical analysis plan, focusing on patients suffering from moderate to severe MDD; and (3) the Phase Two Trials lacked adequate data, particularly in regards to the patient population size and the ratio of male to female patients within the patient population, to be able to accurately predict the results of the KOASTAL-1 study.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Neumora Therapeutics, Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/neumora-therapeutics-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=140874&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Basis flat; farmers eye developing crops
data.description:
Spot basis bids for hard red winter (HRW) wheat held steady in the southern U.S. Plains on Thursday in quiet trade as K.C. HRW wheat futures eked out a higher close and producers continued to monitor the growth of the 2025 crop.

* Some farmers are delaying wheat sales until they get a better idea of the size of their crop, which is typically harvested in June and July.

* Dry conditions persist in much of the southern Plains, and winter wheat is in need of rain. The latest weekly U.S. Drought Monitor map, released on Thursday, showed "moderate drought" across 59% of top wheat producer Kansas, up from 57% the previous week.

* The U.S. Department of Agriculture is scheduled to release its first national crop progress report of 2025, including condition ratings for U.S. winter wheat, on Monday, April 7.

* The USDA reported export sales of U.S. old-crop wheat in the week ended March 27 at 340,000 metric tons, above a range of trade expectations. The figure included 181,900 tons of HRW wheat. Weekly sales of new-crop wheat totaled 95,200 tons, including 10,600 tons of HRW wheat.

* K.C. May HRW wheat futures (KWK25) settled up 1/2 cent at $5.69 a bushel, supported by short-covering and worries about Plains dryness. However, worries about an expanding trade war slowing global demand for U.S. goods pressured commodity and equity markets.

data.title: ZI INVESTIGATION ALERT: Johnson Fistel PLLP Investigates the Directors and Officers of ZoomInfo Technologies for Breach of Fiduciary Duties
data.description:
SAN DIEGO, April 03, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Johnson Fistel, PLLP announces that it is investigating whether certain directors and officers of ZoomInfo Technologies, Inc. breached their fiduciary duties to ZoomInfo and its shareholders.

What can I do?   If you are a current long term ZoomInfo shareholder, you may have legal claims that may be brought on behalf of the company, against the company’s directors and officers. If you wish to discuss this notice or your legal rights, please contact lead analyst Jim Baker (jimb@johnsonfistel.com) at 619-814-4471. If emailing, please include a phone number.

If you have continuously owned ZoomInfo shares, you can click or copy and paste the link below in a browser to join: https://www.cognitoforms.com/JohnsonFistel/ZoomInfoTechnologiesInc

What is this about? Previously a shareholder class action complaint was filed against the company. According to the complaint, during the class period, defendants failed to disclose to investors: (a) that ZoomInfo’s financial and operational results during the Class Period had been temporarily inflated by the ephemeral effects of the COVID-19 pandemic, which had pulled-forward demand for the Company’s database of digital contact information; (b) that material portions of ZoomInfo’s existing customer base were attempting to either substantially reduce their use of the Company’s product or abandon it altogether; (c) that ZoomInfo had used manipulative and coercive auto-renew policies and threats of litigation to force customers into remaining with the Company for an additional contractual term even though they did not want to; (d) that ZoomInfo’s coercive customer retention tactics had materially damaged the Company’s customer relationships, client franchise, and competitive advantages, and created a hidden demand cliff for costumer contract renewals in future periods; (e) that ZoomInfo’s reported accounts receivable were materially comprised of debts owed by high-risk small business customers that had a high likelihood of non-payment and had been induced to transact with the Company through a credit program the Company implemented in 2022; (f) that ZoomInfo’s allowance for credit losses was materially inadequate and understated the risk of non-payments by the Company’s customers; and (g) that as a result of (a)–(f), above, ZoomInfo’s reported revenues, operating income, and customer and retention metrics were materially overstated.

Johnson Fistel PLLP’s investigation seeks to determine whether ZoomInfo senior officers or members of its board of directors harmed the company by breaching their fiduciary duties or otherwise violating securities laws in connection with the foregoing alleged conduct.

About Johnson Fistel, PLLP | Top Law Firm, Securities Fraud, Investors Rights:

Johnson Fistel, PLLP is a nationally recognized shareholder rights law firm with offices in California, New York, Georgia, Idaho, and Colorado. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in shareholder derivative and securities class action lawsuits. We also extend our services to foreign investors who have purchased on US exchanges. Stay updated with news on stock drops and learn how Johnson Fistel, PLLP can help you recover your losses. For more information about the firm and its attorneys, please visit http://www.johnsonfistel.com.

Achievements: In 2024, Johnson Fistel was honored to be ranked in the Top 10 Plaintiff Law Firms by the ISS Securities Class Action Services. This recognition underscores our effectiveness in advocating for investors, having recovered approximately $90,725,000 for aggrieved clients in cases where we served as lead or co-lead counsel. This notable accomplishment marks the eighth occasion our firm has been recognized as a top plaintiffs’ securities law firm in the United States, as determined by the total dollar value of final recoveries.

Attorney advertising.

Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

Services may be performed by attorneys in any of our offices.

Johnson Fistel, PLLP has paid for the dissemination of this promotional communication, and Frank J. Johnson is the attorney responsible for its content.

KIRILL DMITRIEV, RUSSIAN ENVOY, IS INTERVIEWED ON FOX NEWS
data.description:
TRANSCRIPT

April 03, 2025

NEWS PROGRAM

KIRILL DMITRIEV, RUSSIAN ENVOY

KIRILL DMITRIEV, RUSSIAN ENVOY, IS INTERVIEWED ON FOX NEWS

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

20 East Thomas Road, Suite 2200

Phoenix, AZ 85012

asc.info@viqsolutions.com

Copyright 2025. Provided under license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

All materials herein are protected by United States copyright law

and/or license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc., and may not be

reproduced, distributed, transmitted, displayed, published or

broadcast without the prior written permission of

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

You may not alter or remove any trademark, copyright or other

notice from copies of the content.

KIRILL DMITRIEV, RUSSIAN ENVOY, IS INTERVIEWED ON FOX NEWS

APRIL 3, 2025

SPEAKERS:

KIRILL DMITRIEV, RUSSIAN ENVOY

(BEGIN VIDEO CLIP)

DONALD TRUMP, PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES: We have an envoy from Russia. We're talking about it. We'd like to see that stop as soon as possible, because thousands of people are being killed a week. We're, you know, spearheading the drive to get it done.

Europe has not been successful in dealing with President Putin, but I think I will be successful.

(END VIDEO CLIP)

BRET BAIER, FOX NEWS ANCHOR: That was a short time ago on Air Force One, President Trump saying he believes Russian leader Vladimir Putin wants to reach a deal to stop the war in Ukraine. He also said he believes the Ukrainian president is there too, wants a deal soon.

The administration is making another push to end the conflict right now. The president's special envoy is talking with Russian negotiator Kirill Dmitriev, who joins us now live on set.

Thank you so much for being here.

KIRILL DMITRIEV, RUSSIAN ENVOY: Thanks for having me, Bret.

BAIER: How do you characterize the talks right now and how they're going?

DMITRIEV: I think President Trump administration has met — has made tremendous progress. There was no dialogue with Russia in Biden administration for the last three years.

There was no trying to understand Russian position. There was no real solutions that could have been successful. And what President Trump team has done, they understood what the solution space may be and they achieved the first de-escalation ever in the conflict, which is stopping hits on energy infrastructure between Russia and Ukraine.

So we are having good discussions. Our diplomatic people are also discussing possible outcomes, but there is no question that President Trump team not only stopped World War III from happening, but also had already achieved sizable progress on Ukraine resolution.

BAIER: After that resolution, though, of the energy infrastructure, there were attacks by the Russians on the Ukrainians' energy infrastructure.

DMITRIEV: But those were unfortunately attacks by Ukrainian on lots of Russian infrastructure, which are not too much reported in the press. And that, of course, has to stop.

And I think energy infrastructure cease-fire has to hold.

BAIER: You, Russia, have a lot of sanctions put on your country by the U.S.

And it runs the gamut, importation of Russian oil, energy products into the U.S., sanctions on oil-carrying vessels, other sectors, financial services, aerospace, metals and mining. In fact, you had to get a waiver on sanctions to travel here to the U.S.

DMITRIEV: That's right. Yes. Yes. Yes.

BAIER: Is lifting sanctions part of the condition for the cease-fire that you're talking about?

DMITRIEV: Well, Russia is not asking for lifting of sanctions, because if you look at our GDP growth, it's actually 4 percent last year versus just 1 percent in Europe.

If you look at how debt-to-GDP, it's 18 percent versus 100 percent in Europe. So I think what we see is that actually U.S. companies lost around $300 billion of foregone profit from withdrawing from Russia. So I think sanctions may be lifted when U.S. companies want to go back, want to take advantage of business opportunities in Russia.

But, right now, Russia is not making any preconditions, not asking for specific sanction relief.

BAIER: There is — there are the sanctions, but there still is some trade with Russia, if you look at the numbers, $526.1 million exports to Russia, $3 billion imports from Russia. It is down significantly with all the sanctions.

Were you surprised that Russia was not on the tariff list announced by President Trump yesterday?

DMITRIEV: Well, I think it was more of a technical issue for...

BAIER: Not part of this negotiation?

DMITRIEV: ... Trump administration, not a big part or not a part of this negotiation too much.

But I think there is basically huge opportunity for us to have dialogue and to do business together. And, by the way, we talk to many people in the administration, some people in the legislative branch, and they always, once you ask them, hey, how about Russia and U.S. doing more business, restoring relationship, they actually are surprised and say, well, maybe that's a good idea.

So I think there is a positive outcome of not only Ukraine discussions, but of restoring ties and having lots of partnership and economic opportunities between U.S. and Russia.

BAIER: President Zelenskyy sat in that very seat a couple of weeks ago.

DMITRIEV: Yes, in that program, yes.

BAIER: ... and told me that his main concern for Ukraine and his people is security guarantees. Take a listen.

(BEGIN VIDEO CLIP)

BAIER: President Trump is convinced that you will never sign a deal with Vladimir Putin. Is he right?

VOLODYMYR ZELENSKYY, UKRAINIAN PRESIDENT: No, I think so. What I think Ukraine wants peace. And we will have — we will have any way diplomacy. We will have negotiations.

I only said that I think so it's about just and lasting peace. We have to be in strong position. What does it mean? Just to know that our army is strong, that our partners are with us and that we have security guarantees.

(BEGIN VIDEO CLIP)

BAIER: So is there any security guarantee that Ukraine would be OK with that Russia would accept?

DMITRIEV: And, again, Bret, I think I focused a lot on investment economics, so I can just more hypothetically talk about the specific issue.

But I think Ukraine joining NATO, as our president said, is not at all possible. And that I think has been widely accepted, including by Trump administration. Some security guarantees in some form may be acceptable. And I think...

BAIER: But you can say definitively that your president, President Putin, wants to get to a deal?

DMITRIEV: Well, I can say so, and he said so publicly. And he wants a permanent peace in Ukraine.

And I think he actually publicly said what some of those conditions are. And I think President Trump administration has also been very vocal in our discussion. They want a fair deal. But in lots of negotiations, they actually push for strong Ukraine. They are supporting strong position of Ukraine. So they want a fair deal, but they want Ukraine to be strong.

BAIER: I want to ask you about the economics in a second.

But the U.S.-European commander was asked about Russia's intentions if they get a deal. Take a listen.

(BEGIN VIDEO CLIP)

SEN. ELISSA SLOTKIN (D-MI): Do you believe that, after Russia invaded Georgia, then Crimea, then Eastern Ukraine, that if some sort of deal was reached with Vladimir Putin, especially one that advantaged him, that he'd be done with invading neighboring countries, that he would be done with adventurism in Europe?

GEN. CHRISTOPHER CAVOLI, COMMANDER, U.S. EUROPEAN COMMAND: I do not, ma'am. My professional assessment based on years of study is that this is part of a broader pattern in Russian history.

(END VIDEO CLIP)

BAIER: If Russia gets a deal, makes a deal, is that the end of the adventurism going forward to other countries?

DMITRIEV: Well, I think definitely so.

And I would say there are lots of false and fake narratives circulating, such as Russia possibly going into Europe. I mean, that's just a crazy idea. And the problem is, there are lots of crazy people, and they discuss it together, so it becomes like crazy, group crazy.

And I think President Trump is very realistic. He knows it's not going to happen. And I think it's very important that there are safeguards in place. But what is dangerous is that there are lots of sort of false narratives and people stay in those narratives. Before, the same people were proposing decisions on Ukraine that would never have worked.

And if you propose decisions that doesn't work, it means you don't actually have a decision that can work.

BAIER: Last thing. You say that the Russian economy is good. There's obviously a lot of other people saying it's not and it's hurting.

Is one of your messages here that, whatever happens, there could be an economic relationship between the Russians and the U.S.?

DMITRIEV: Definitely.

And we can do so many great things together in Arctic. We can do deals in rare earth and other minerals. We can do lots of cooperation in LNG and other areas. So I think one of the big issues between us, we never had really lots of economic cooperation. We believe that economic cooperation will allow us to also solve any political issues and be positive.

And I think that positive economic cooperation can happen with President Trump team.

BAIER: Covering all sides, we appreciate you coming in and talking to our viewers.

DMITRIEV: Thank you, Bret.

From: Todd Stephens (tstephens@viqsolutions.com) Sent: Thursday, April 3, 2025 5:17 PM To: Smith, Connor (Connor.Smith@FOX.COM); Kimberly Tharp (ktharp@viqsolutions.com);

ASC News Team (asc.newsteam@viqsolutions.com) Subject: [EXTERNAL] Austin Metcalf's Mom & Brother

THE INFORMATION CONTAINED IN RUSH AND PREFEED TRANSCRIPTS IS A BEST POSSIBLE TEXTUAL REPRESENTATION OF THE APPLICABLE CONTENT. WHILE EFFORTS ARE MADE TO PROVIDE AN ACCURATE TRANSCRIPTION, THERE MAY BE MATERIAL ERRORS, OMISSIONS, OR INACCURACIES IN THE REPORTING OF THE SUBSTANCE OF THE RUSH AND PREFEED TRANSCRIPTS FILES DUE TO AUDIO IMPAIRMENT

WILL CAIN, FOX NEWS ANCHOR: It's a terrifying, gut-wrenching, incomprehensible scene in Frisco, Texas, just 20 miles north of where I sit here today.

A high school track meet yesterday turned deadly; 17-year-old football star Austin Metcalf was stabbed in the heart and left to die in his twin brother's arms after a fight broke out yesterday. The suspect was allegedly told he was sitting in the wrong seat in the stands of the stadium before drawing a knife and attacking Austin.

Police have yet to reveal what led up to that moment, but we do know the other student has been charged with murder.

Joining me now is Austin's mother, Meghan Metcalf, and his twin brother, Hunter.

Meghan, Hunter, thank you for being with me today. I can't imagine how difficult of a day. And I just — I have to start with how sorry I am of what you're going through this morning, this afternoon.

MEGHAN METCALF, MOTHER OF AUSTIN METCALF: Thank you.

CAIN: I think I'd like to just start — I'd love to just start with, tell me about Hunter, if you could, Meghan. Tell me about your son.

HUNTER METCALF, TWIN BROTHER OF AUSTIN METCALF: I can speak for her for a little bit, since she calms down just a little bit.

But, as to me, I know I'm a little more spontaneous. I like to do stuff all around, and just get out of line sometimes. And Austin's always there to check on me and keep me straight, so I wouldn't be in trouble and do anything like that.

CAIN: Hunter and Meghan, whenever you feel ready, jump into the conversation.

M. METCALF: All right.

CAIN: If you never feel ready, that's completely understandable to me and everybody else watching.

Hunter, thanks so much for being able to do this today. Just a little more about your brother Austin. From what I understand, I think he has it in his social media bio, 3.96 GPA student, certainly definitely into football. And it looked — when I was looking into him last night, he was very interested in being recruited to play football in college.

H. METCALF: Yes, sir. He was very highly recruited. Just coming out of his junior year, he's had a really great year.

And everyone on the team, coaches, everyone near around the area just knew that he was about to do big things in football. And besides his GPA, man, I think he was extremely smart, always in the books. He just — it came to him really easy, and just him playing football as well just made him just a great person overall.

CAIN: Meghan, we will try this again, both of us, and we will see how far we get.

When I read your story last night, obviously, this is not about me. I have a 17-year-old son. I live 20 miles south of you. My son's in Frisco every night playing soccer, and we have a lot of friends of friends who knew Austin. So the circles got tight, and this hit really close to home. And I'm just so sorry for you.

Can you explain us at all today how you feel? It's just got to be so — the word I keep coming back to, Meghan, is incomprehensible. How could this happen at a track meet.

M. METCALF: It is.

He just took my — my son is gone. And I have no — I don't really have words. I have tried to figure out what I was going to say. But he's just going to be so missed by mostly our family, but this whole community has really wrapped their arms around us.

And if I could say anything to the nation, I would say to make sure that you just hug your babies tight, because you don't know when is the last time you get to do it.

CAIN: Hunter, I know you were there.

Thank you, Meghan. Take your time.

H. METCALF: Yes, sir.

CAIN: Hunter, I know you were there when it happened. What can you tell us about what happened at that track meet? How did this — how could this thing that seems like is not on the menu of possibilities could happen?

Again, I was at a track meet, by the way, on Tuesday. This is not something you think could ever happen. How did it happen?

H. METCALF: So, there was a — it was a really bad storm in the morning, and they had a lot of delays.

And me and my brother a couple of kids were walking back from the field house of the middle school, because we attended that middle school when we went to middle school. And we were saying hello to old coaches and catching up with them.

And then we're walking back to our stand. We put our tent up, so all the bleachers were right there. We're not wet from all the rain. We come back. We sit down. And this kid says something behind me. I have never met this kid my entire life. I never knew he was.

Said something just inappropriate and uncalled for. When I turned around and asked him who he was, he started getting really aggressive. My brother stepped in, and he started being how he is always is being just brothers, just having my back. And things started getting more, like, higher intensity.

And the kid said some, like, aggressive stuff, like, reckless stuff. Austin tried to handle the problem and...

CAIN: And...

M. METCALF: Austin was always one to step up. He always was one to step up and be the person that would do the right thing. And he was doing the right thing by saying to this gentleman that you're not supposed to be in this tent.

This is — like, because they have personal items there.

H. METCALF: A lot of stuff gets stolen, just like that, just precautions and stuff.

And our coaches always told us, don't let all the schools mess with our stuff. We just sit in our tent area. And we set that up for the girls track every meet we had. So it's just like — and he knew he wasn't supposed to be there. And it was just a tragedy, what happened.

CAIN: And then what happened to Austin? After the — after your brother stepped up, what happened then?

H. METCALF: He stepped up over there, asked him to move. I guess he grabbed the bag.

And then I whipped my head around. And then, all of a sudden, I see him running down the bleachers grabbing his chest. He just — rushed and helped. He said, he got stabbed, he got stabbed. First, I froze for half-a-second. I didn't know what to do.

And then I saw him kind of fall. I would go over there. I grabbed him. I see...

(CROSSTALK)

CAIN: Yes, I understand, best I can.

H. METCALF: Yes, there was — you can see — you can picture what happened, what was coming out of his chest.

CAIN: Yes. Yes.

H. METCALF: And you all can visualize all that stuff. I don't want to put that on my mom right now, but I put my hand — I pushed my hand on there to try and make it stop. And I grabbed his head and I looked into his eyes.

I just — I just saw his soul leave, and it took my soul too.

CAIN: I'm so sorry.

I'm going to ask you one last question. I have seen interviews with your husband, who I can hear on the side, by the way. I think he's there in the room with you.

M. METCALF: No, but...

CAIN: I saw an interview last night. OK, someone else. Yes, OK.

H. METCALF: Yes.

(CROSSTALK)

CAIN: But I have seen interviews with your husband in the last 24 hours. And it was remarkable to me that I heard your husband say within hours of this that he forgave this other young man, that he forgave this other young man, and he got there so quickly.

And I honestly don't know that I could. And I just want to see. And I don't know that there's a right or a wrong place to be, especially as quickly as this has happened.

Meghan, how do you feel on that front today?

M. METCALF: I am not like their dad. I am so angry at that boy. And it's just — it's just not fair. That's all I have.

CAIN: I understand. It's not fair.

H. METCALF: All I'd like to say is forgave, not forgotten, but I'm not at that point yet to forgive that kid for what he did to my brother, because 17 years, my best friend just there, and in the blink of an eye, just I lost him.

So I'm not at that point to forgive him forgive and — forgive and forget. But, eventually, I will. But I just — I pray for his family. Just I pray that they understand what we're through, is why I just pray for everybody.

CAIN: I totally understand we're both of you sit today on that front. I don't know that I could either.

That's why I found it remarkable when I heard that from your husband, but everyone has to go through this their own way. I just pray that God goes through this with you both and your husband and the rest of your family.

And I really appreciate you sharing this story with me and the rest of the nation.

Meghan, Hunter, thank you. Our prayers remain with you.

H. METCALF: Thank you, sir.

M. METCALF: Thank you.

CAIN: All right.

There is a GoFundMe, by the way, for the family of Austin Metcalf that is up now, which you can go and check out there to help his family heal.

Austin Metcalf was 17 years old.

END

data.title: Law Firm Kirkland & Ellis in Talks with White House to Avoid Executive Order
data.description:
By Erin Mulvaney, Josh Dawsey and C. Ryan Barber

Kirkland & Ellis, the largest U.S. law firm by revenue, is in talks with the White House to avoid an executive order similar to those levied against several of its rivals, according to people familiar with the matter.

The firm, which has some 4,000 lawyers and brought in about $9 billion last year, is one of the White House's next targets for sanctions, some of the people said. If President Trump moves forward with an executive order, it would mark an escalation of the administration's battle against giants in the legal industry.

Kirkland — a powerhouse in the worlds of private equity and mergers and acquisitions — has been eager to avoid a conflict with the White House and has hired a lobbyist as part of its efforts, the people said. It isn't clear what objections Trump and his inner circle would have to the firm's activities.

The president's executive orders so far have generally threatened lawyers' access to federal buildings, as well as government contracts held by their clients.

Three targeted firms, Perkins Coie, Jenner & Block and WilmerHale, filed lawsuits challenging the administration's actions. Three different judges have blocked the administration from enforcing the bulk of the orders.

Other firms have instead elected to strike deals with the administration, including Kirkland's major corporate law peers, Paul Weiss and Skadden, which agreed to provide tens of millions of dollars of pro bono legal services to support administration initiatives, including support for veterans.

The settling firms have viewed the agreements as a low-cost way to obtain peace with the administration and avoid complications for their businesses. But the settlements have drawn objections from some lawyers — both within the firms and in the broader legal community — who view the deals as capitulating to bullying tactics by the president.

A number of Kirkland lawyers took top roles in the first Trump administration. Among them were Jeffrey Rosen, who left the firm to serve as deputy transportation secretary and later deputy attorney general, and Brian Benczkowski, who led the Justice Department's criminal division and then returned to the firm.

Another former Kirkland partner, Jeffrey Clark, headed the Justice Department's environmental and natural resources division and sought to aid Trump's efforts to overturn the 2020 election results.

Kirkland was one of 20 firms recently questioned by the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, which requested data about firms' diversity, equity, and inclusion practices. The Trump-appointed interim chair Andrea Lucas said these firms' diversity programs could be illegal under federal civil rights laws.

Write to Erin Mulvaney at erin.mulvaney@wsj.com, Josh Dawsey at Joshua.Dawsey@WSJ.com and C. Ryan Barber at ryan.barber@wsj.com

data.title: Champion Iron Target Price Cut 13% to A$6.20/Share by Bell Potter>CIA.AU
Glencore seeks Australian government bailout for local copper assets
data.description:
Anglo-Swiss commodities giant Glencore said that it has approached both Queensland's and the Federal Government of Australia about the future of its Mount Isa copper smelter and Townville copper refinery assets.

In October 2023, Glencore first announced the closure of its Mount Isa copper mines in the second half of 2025, citing studies and reviews stating it was not possible to further extend the life of the underground mines.

"Through the work undertaken we now expect this (impact on workforce) to be about 500 people and we're actively working to redeploy as many people as possible over the coming months," Sam Strohmayr, chief operating officer for the Swiss miner's Australian zinc and copper assets, said on Wednesday in an address to Mount Isa's local communities, adding that the original estimate was around 1,200 roles.

"We are assessing the future of our copper processing assets against a backdrop of the largest drops in treatment and refining charges in 25 years, with smelters in countries like China and Indonesia heavily subsidised by their governments," Strohmayr added.

With this announcement, Glencore becomes the second international commodities firm to reconsider the status of its Australian assets, following Swiss trading house Trafigura's decision to place its struggling Nyrstar zinc smelter in Tasmania under strategic review.

Speaking about the decision in a late March onstage interview at the FT Commodities Global Summit, Trafigura's CEO, Richard Holtum, said mineral processing facilities such as smelters should be considered "a national security issue", adding that unless governments provided more support, the West would never reduce its dependence on China for supplies of critical minerals.

data.title: Goodcom Asset Offers Shareholder Perks for Fukuoka Listing
data.description:
Good Com Asset will offer digital gifts to shareholders after its Fukuoka Stock Exchange listing, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

Shareholders with 500 to 999 shares as of May and October will receive 20,000 yen per period, while those with 1,000 or more shares will get 50,000 yen.

Eligible gifts include Amazon and QUO card pay vouchers.

The company aims to expand in Kyushu and boost investor engagement. It maintains a 35% dividend payout target and will consider buybacks.

data.title: Genusplus Target Price Raised 3.1% to A$3.30/Share by Bell Potter>GNP.AU
QBE Insurance Target Price Cut 0.9% to A$23.00/Share by Macquarie>QBE.AU
StemRIM Secures China Patent for Redasemtide Regenerative Drug Candidate
data.description:
StemRIM secured a patent in China for Redasemtide, its regenerative medicine candidate, expanding its potential use in treating traumatic articular cartilage deficiency syndrome, osteoarthritis, and osteochondritis dissecans, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The Osaka-based company, which develops therapies leveraging the body's natural regenerative abilities, said the patent strengthens Redasemtide's development prospects in China.

data.title: Colombia central bank chief says board members do not represent any political faction
data.description:
Colombia central bank chief Leonardo Villar said on Thursday that members of the bank's seven-person board, with the exception of the country's finance minister, do not represent any political faction.

"None of the members of the board, excepting the finance minister, is a representative in their heart of a government or of opposition parties," Villar told a pension funds conference in the city of Cartagena. His comments followed months of repeated pressure from President Gustavo Petro for the board to lower the benchmark interest rate and comments from the president that some members belong to opposition parties.

The board on Monday held the benchmark rate steady for the second consecutive time on inflation and external concerns.

Monday marked the first interest rate votes by Laura Moisa-Elicabide and Cesar Giraldo, who were recently appointed by Petro, and by new Finance Minister German Avila, who told journalists last week he would opt for a rate cut.

Minutes from the meeting showed that while a minority of the board proposed a middle-ground 25-basis-point reduction in the rate, that was rejected and three board members ultimately voted unsuccessfully for a 50-basis-point cut.

A memorandum said the proposal was made "with the objective of giving signs of support to the economic recovery, without neglecting the necessary caution in the face of the international situation and the fiscal challenges."

data.title: MIXI to Sell Decollte Stake to IBJ for 609 Million Yen
data.description:
MIXI will sell 1,353,800 shares of Decollte Holdings to IBJ Inc. for 609.2 million yen, dissolving its capital and business alliance with the company, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The transaction, set for April 10, will reduce MIXI's stake in Decollte to 3.15% from 27.03%.

The company said it will maintain a cooperative relationship with Decollte despite the sale.

data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 3, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your SoundHound AI, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/soundhound-ai-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=140872&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against SoundHound AI, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between May 10, 2024 and March 3, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) the material weaknesses in SoundHound's internal controls over financial reporting impaired the Company's ability to effectively account for corporate acquisitions; (ii) in addition, the Company overstated the extent to which it had remediated, and/or its ability to remediate, the material weaknesses in its internal controls over financial reporting; (iii) as a result of the foregoing material weaknesses, SoundHound's reported goodwill following the Amelia Acquisition was inflated and would need to be corrected; (iv) further, SoundHound would likely require extra time and expense to effectively account for the SYNQ3 and Amelia Acquisitions; (v) the foregoing increased the risk that the Company would be unable to timely file certain financial reports with the SEC; and (vi) as a result, the Company's public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in SoundHound AI, Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/soundhound-ai-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=140872&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. data.title: Did Aldeyra Therapeutics, Inc. Mislead Investors? Shareholder Rights Advocates at Levi & Korsinsky Investigate– ADLX
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 3, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Aldeyra Therapeutics, Inc. ("Aldeyra Therapeutics, Inc.") (NASDAQ:ADLX) concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On April 3, 2025, The company disclosed that the FDA issued a second Complete Response Letter (CRL) for reproxalap, Aldeyra's lead dry eye disease drug candidate. The FDA's CRL cited a failure to demonstrate efficacy for reproxalap and flagged concerns with the design of Aldeyra's most recent trial, including differences in baseline symptom scores that may have impacted the reliability of their trial results.

Following this news, shares of Aldeyra Therapeutics, Inc. fell over 74% in intraday trading. To obtain additional information, go to:

data.title: Ansell to Offset New US Tariffs by Raising Prices; Maintains Fiscal 2025 Earnings Guidance
data.description:
Ansell plans to offset the Trump administration's new tariffs by raising its prices, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company, which makes surgical gloves and gowns, generates roughly 43% of its revenue in the US. It has factories and third-party suppliers in Malaysia, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam, and China, which are all subject to the new US tariff policy announced on Thursday.

Ansell claims no country supplies more than 30% of its imports into the US, with its largest exposures being to Malaysia and Sri Lanka, per the filing.

Ansell maintained its guidance range for fiscal 2025 adjusted earnings at $1.18 to $1.28 per share.
